title: Implement SOCKS5 tunnel in open_authenticated_ws
description:
When proxy is Some, create a SOCKS5 tunnel using tokio-socks before the WebSocket/TLS upgrade in open_authenticated_ws().

## What
The actual SOCKS5 proxy implementation: parse the proxy URL, create an async SOCKS5 TCP stream to the relay host, then layer TLS and WebSocket on top of that stream.

## Algorithm
1. At the top of open_authenticated_ws(), if proxy.is_some():
   a. Parse proxy URL with url::Url::parse(&proxy_url)?
   b. Extract proxy host (url.host_str().ok_or(anyhow!('missing proxy host'))?) and port (url.port().ok_or(anyhow!('missing proxy port'))?)
   c. Parse relay URL to extract relay host and port (parse with url::Url::parse(url)? since url is the relay URL)
   d. Call: let socks_stream = tokio_socks::tcp::Socks5Stream::connect((proxy_host, proxy_port), (relay_host, relay_port as u16)).await.with_context(|| 'SOCKS5 connect failed')?
   e. If relay scheme is wss:// AND accept_invalid_certs is false:
      - Build TlsConnector::new()?
      - tls_stream = connector.connect_async(relay_host, socks_stream).await?
      - ws = client_async_tls_with_config(url, MaybeTlsStream::NativeTls(tls_stream), None, None).await?.0
   f. If relay scheme is wss:// AND accept_invalid_certs is true:
      - Build TlsConnector with danger_accept_invalid_certs(true)
      - Same as above
   g. If relay scheme is ws://:
      - ws = client_async(url, socks_stream.into_inner()).await?.0  (or client_async_with_config)
2. If proxy.is_none(), use existing code path unchanged.
3. After splitting ws, proceed with the existing auth handshake.

## Type notes (from API research)
- tokio_socks::tcp::Socks5Stream::connect returns Socks5Stream<TcpStream>
- Socks5Stream<TcpStream> implements AsyncRead + AsyncWrite + Unpin
- IntoTargetAddr accepts (&str, u16) — for socks5h pass relay_host as &str; for socks5 resolve first
- native_tls::TlsConnector::connect_async takes domain: &str + stream: impl AsyncRead + AsyncWrite
- Resulting TlsStream<Socks5Stream<TcpStream>> also implements AsyncRead + AsyncWrite
- client_async_tls_with_config accepts any stream impl AsyncRead + AsyncWrite + Unpin + Send + 'static
- The socks5h vs socks5 distinction: if scheme is 'socks5h', pass relay_host as &str directly to Socks5Stream (proxy resolves DNS). If scheme is 'socks5', resolve relay_host locally first to SocketAddr.

## Defensive programming rules
- Every ? must include with_context() describing which step failed
- Never log the proxy URL or relay host at info level in the proxy path
- Never unwrap() in the proxy path — all errors are typed anyhow::Error variants
- The socks5h path must pass relay_host as a string (&str, port) tuple to IntoTargetAddr
- The socks5 path must resolve relay_host locally (tokio::net::lookup_host or similar) before passing to IntoTargetAddr as SocketAddr
- accept_invalid_certs applies to the TLS layer, not to SOCKS5 — SOCKS5 connection itself has no cert

## Agent team
Researcher (re-read transport.rs + tokio-socks API), Implementer A, Implementer B (independent), Tester (derive expected from tokio-socks README + RFC 1928), DIFF (reconcile both impls)
Use critical-path 5-agent team because a wrong implementation silently bypasses the proxy without error.

## Tests
- happy_path_socks5h: connect through a mock SOCKS5 proxy on 127.0.0.1 to a test relay — verify relay sees connection
- no_proxy: verify existing direct connect path still works (no regression)
- bad_proxy_host: proxy URL with unreachable host → returns Err with 'SOCKS5 connect failed' in message
- socks5_vs_socks5h: verify socks5h passes hostname string (not IP) to proxy; verify socks5 resolves IP locally
Acceptance: cargo test --workspace exits 0; clippy exits 0; all four test cases pass.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace exits 0; clippy exits 0; SOCKS5 proxy path is exercised in tests
